factors.summary: Pricing power seems stable as the decrease in sales was offset by increased equity earnings rather than pricing adjustments.

evidence.text: Space Systems operating profit in the second quarter of 2016 increased $46 million, or 16 percent, compared to the same period in 2015. The increase was primarily attributable to approximately $80 million of increased equity earnings in joint ventures (primarily ULA).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is stable as the decrease in sales was offset by increased equity earnings.

factors.summary: Revenue mix shifted towards higher-margin joint ventures, benefiting operating margins.

evidence.text: Total equity earnings recognized by Space Systems (primarily ULA) represented approximately $120 million, or 35 percent, and approximately $170 million, or 29 percent, of this business segments operating profit for the quarter and six months ended June 26, 2016...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifted towards higher-margin joint ventures, benefiting operating margins.

factors.summary: Government satellite programs are experiencing a decline, while strategic missile systems and commercial satellite programs are mixed.

evidence.text: Space Systems net sales during the quarter ended June 26, 2016 decreased $71 million, or 3 percent, compared to the same period in 2015. The decrease was primarily attributable to lower net sales of approximately $115 million for government satellite programs due to decreased volume...

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Government satellite programs are declining, while other areas are mixed.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different segments, with some areas experiencing improvement while others face declines due to integration costs and lower sales volumes.

evidence.text: Aeronautics' operating margin was stable. IS&GS saw an improvement in operating margin. MFC experienced a decline in operating margin. MST had a decline in operating margin due to integration costs.

The increase in product sales at MST was attributable to sales from Sikorsky which was acquired in the fourth quarter of 2015. The increase at Aeronautics was primarily attributable to the F-35 program due to increased volume partially offset by decreased aircraft deliveries (C-5 program).
As a result, operating margin is expected to decline slightly between the years.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with some improvements and some declines.

factors.summary: The company's organic growth is mixed across its segments, with some areas experiencing increases in net sales and operating profit, while others face declines. Factors contributing to this mixed performance include the addition of Sikorsky Aircraft and increased volumes on certain programs, offset by declines in government satellite programs and other business segments.

evidence.text: Aeronautics' net sales increased while operating profit also increased. IS&GS experienced a decline in net sales and operating profit. MFC saw net sales increase but operating profit decreased. MST had significant net sales growth but operating profit decreased.
The projected growth is driven by the addition of Sikorsky Aircraft and increased volume expected on the F-35 program, partially offset by volume declines in three of our five business segments.

We expect Space Systems net sales to decline in the mid-single digit percentage range in 2016 as compared to 2015, primarily driven by program lifecycles and volume on government satellite programs...

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed organic growth across segments with some increases and some declines.

factors.summary: Program execution is encountering significant challenges across multiple projects, including delays and cost overruns in certain contracts, as well as issues related to funding, supplier performance, and testing within the F-35 program. These challenges indicate a mixed performance with ongoing difficulties that require re-evaluation and adjustments.

evidence.text: The company is re-evaluating the scope, estimated costs, and viability of the integrated air and missile defense system program due to performance issues.

Current program challenges include, but are not limited to, supplier and partner performance, software development, level of cost associated with life cycle operations and sustainment and warranties...

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant challenges and delays in program execution across projects.

factors.summary: Volume trends across the company's segments show a mixed performance, with increases in Aeronautics, Mission Systems and Training (MST), and strategic missile systems, offset by declines in Space Systems and Information Systems & Global Solutions (IS&GS).

evidence.text: Aeronautics reported higher net sales due to increased volume on F-35 and C-130 programs. IS&GS saw lower net sales due to program completions and increased competition. MFC had mixed volume trends with some programs increasing and others decreasing.

Product sales increased $888 million, or 9.7%, in the quarter ended June 26, 2016 compared to the same period in 2015, primarily due to increased product sales of about $890 million at MST and about $165 million at Aeronautics, partially offset by decreased products sales of about $95 million at Space Systems and $75 million at IS&GS.

Space Systems net sales during the six months ended June 26, 2016 decreased $165 million, or 4 percent, compared to the same period in 2015. The decrease was attributable to lower net sales of approximately $245 million for government satellite programs due to decreased volume...
